title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, from Chelsea, faces multiple charges for assaulting two children, including using harmful objects.
He allegedly used a cigarette lighter on a 2-year-old and a heated fork on a 3-year-old, causing injuries.
Bautista-Zepeda was found in Virginia and held without bail after fleeing Massachusetts; a hearing is set for May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, was convicted of raping a 20-year-old woman in a Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023.
Jammal was sentenced to 13 ½ to 15 years in prison, followed by 5 years of probation.
The attack occurred after Jammal made a suggestive comment and assaulted the victim in a restaurant hallway.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Plans approved to convert Church of the Open Word in Newtonville into six condos and a new five-story building.
The historic church, built in 1893, was purchased by Habitech Communities for $4 million in 2024.
Greater Boston sees increasing conversions of former churches into residences due to shrinking congregations and housing demand.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard is a potential trade target for the Celtics, offering versatility and championship experience.
LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, could be a fit for the Celtics if looking for a final title run.
John Collins, Jaxson Hayes, and Coby White are other potential offseason additions for the Celtics' roster depth.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advanced to the NCAA Division II World Series, their first time in program history, defeating Molloy University.
Endicott College secured their fourth consecutive Division III championship appearance by winning against Salve Regina University.
Bentley and Endicott's opponents and schedules for the World Series are yet to be announced.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Celtics are set to work out UConn's Alex Karaban next week as part of their pre-draft activities.
Karaban is ranked No. 32 on ESPN's top 100 draft prospects; Celtics hold picks No. 27 and No. 40.
At UConn, Karaban played 151 games, averaging 12.5 points, 5.0 rebounds, and is the all-time leader in several categories.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway on May 22, blending jazz and pop influences.
His setlist included hits from past albums and tracks from his latest album "Whatever’s Clever!" released in March.
Puth engaged the audience with improvisations and music theory insights, highlighting his Berklee College roots.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed their 1996 album 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly.
Jakob Dylan acknowledged the setlist's awkwardness due to the album's front-loaded popular songs.
The band followed their album with a performance of Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark', complementing their own hits.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston experienced the largest population decline in Massachusetts, losing 1,338 residents in the last year.
Everett and Worcester had the highest population growth, with increases of 2,362 and 1,916 residents, respectively.
The state overall grew by 15,500 residents, marking 0.2% growth, slower than nearly 1% in previous years.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments and Market Basket's parent company are on Forbes' list of largest U.S. family businesses.
Fidelity, led by the Johnson family, ranked 14th with $37.7 billion in revenue; Market Basket ranked 69th with $8 billion.
Fidelity and Market Basket highlight Massachusetts' representation in Forbes' rankings of family-run firms, excluding single-generation businesses.
